title: Senior IoT Architects
qualification: BE/B.Tech or ME/M.Tech in Computer Science, Electrical, or Electronics Engineering
requiredSkills: AWS IoT Core / Azure IoT Hub
Edge computing and fog computing architectures
Rust or Go for high-performance services
Security best practices for IoT deployments
rolesAndResponsibilities: Design and architect end-to-end IoT solutions for smart city and logistics deployments
Define cloud ingestion pipelines capable of handling millions of device messages per second
Collaborate with firmware and hardware teams to establish communication protocols and data schemas
Lead architecture reviews, ensuring scalability, reliability, and security across all IoT systems
Mentor senior engineers and drive technical direction for the IoT platform team
Produce detailed technical specifications and architecture decision records (ADRs)

title: Firmware Engineer
qualification: BE in Electronics / Telecommunication / Electrical / Computer Science
requiredSkills: Communication interfaces: SPI, I2C, UART, USB
rolesAndResponsibilities: Develop and maintain firmware for high-throughput RFID scanning endpoints
Implement and optimize communication stacks for BLE, Wi-Fi, and LoRaWAN modules
Write unit and integration tests for embedded software components
Collaborate closely with hardware engineers during bring-up and validation phases
Debug and resolve field issues through log analysis and remote diagnostics

title: Cybersecurity Analyst
qualification: BS/MS in Cybersecurity, Information Security, or Computer Science
requiredSkills: Federal compliance frameworks: NIST 800-53, FedRAMP, CMMC
rolesAndResponsibilities: Implement and monitor zero-trust security protocols across federal agency client deployments
Conduct regular vulnerability scans, penetration tests, and security audits
Respond to and lead incident response for security events across client environments
Produce detailed compliance reports aligned to NIST 800-53 and FedRAMP control families
Brief federal agency stakeholders on threat landscape and remediation posture
Collaborate with DevSecOps teams to embed security into CI/CD pipelines

title: Enterprise Account Executive
qualification: BS in Business, Marketing, or related field (MBA preferred)
requiredSkills: Salesforce CRM and sales analytics platforms
Strategic account planning and multi-threaded relationship management
Complex deal negotiation and contract structuring
Logistics, supply chain, or federal sector vertical knowledge
rolesAndResponsibilities: Drive new logo acquisition and strategic partnership development in the logistics and public sector verticals
Own the full sales cycle from prospecting through contract execution for enterprise accounts
Collaborate with solutions engineers to craft tailored demonstrations and proof-of-concept deployments
Develop and maintain C-suite and VP-level relationships across target accounts
Forecast pipeline accurately in Salesforce and report weekly to VP of Sales
Represent Delonti at industry conferences, trade shows, and executive briefings
